prompt:
Design a TerraOps Offline Environmental & Catalog Intelligence Portal to help plant operators, sustainability analysts, and HR/recruiting teams run a single local workstation site for environmental performance tracking, product master data governance, and talent matching without relying on any internet connectivity. The Yew-based web UI provides role-based workspaces for Administrator, Data Steward, Analyst, Recruiter, and Regular User. Users sign in with a locally validated username and password, then land on KPI dashboards that summarize cycle time, conversion/funnel stages, anomaly counts, and efficiency trends with interactive slice-and-drill by site, department, time window, and category; dashboards support scheduled report generation to local PDF/CSV/Excel and on-screen threshold alerts (for example, flagging when a comfort index exceeds 82 for more than 15 minutes or when SKU on-shelf compliance drops below 95% for a day). Data Stewards manage Product Master Data with SPU/SKU, barcodes, categories, brands, units, sales tax rates by state, shelf life in days, images, and on/off-shelf status, including bulk import/export from Excel/CSV with preview, row-level validation feedback, and a visible change history showing who changed what and when. Analysts configure environmental metric fusion by selecting sources, alignment rules, and confidence labels, then view derived metrics like 15-minute moving averages, rate-of-change, and a comfort index that blends temperature, humidity, and air speed; each chart provides a “why this value” panel that traces the computation lineage. Recruiters and hiring managers use intelligent search to filter and sort candidates or roles by keywords, skills, major, education, experience bands, and availability time, then review recommendation lists with match scores and an explainable match-reason display; users can tune preference weights, maintain watchlists, and provide thumbs-up/down feedback, with a cold-start default that prioritizes recency and completeness until at least 10 feedback events exist.

The backend uses Actix-web to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled Yew frontend, enforcing input validation, pagination defaults (50 rows per page), and deterministic timeouts (3 seconds per request with one retry for idempotent reads) to support poor-network or offline-loopback conditions. PostgreSQL persists product master records, environmental observations, derived metric definitions, computation lineage (including source IDs, alignment parameters, and confidence), user profiles and preference weights, search indices, KPI snapshots, and notification events; all timestamps are stored with timezone and displayed in MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time. Security and compliance safeguards require TLS even on local LAN, encrypted-at-rest storage for sensitive fields (password hashes using Argon2id; optional masking for PII such as partial email display if stored), short-lived access tokens (15 minutes) with refresh via local session, and hotlink protection for images using signed URLs that expire after 10 minutes. Endpoint allowlists and certificate pinning can be configured per deployment to restrict which internal devices can connect. Privacy controls allow administrators to set retention policies, such as purging raw environmental readings after 18 months while keeping aggregated KPIs for 5 years, and deleting user feedback after 24 months of inactivity. The notification center is fully local: events (entry deadlines, review results, publish completion, anomaly alerts) generate in-app messages with read/unread state, user subscriptions, and a retry queue for transient failures; “email” delivery is implemented only as exportable local mailbox files for on-device clients, with no outbound sending. Offline support includes tiered caching of static resources and images, incremental loading for long lists, placeholder states when data is unavailable, and built-in monitoring dashboards for API latency, error rate, and client crash reports stored locally for administrators to review.
